perturbation.original_text: Pursuant and subject to Rule 13d-1(k)(1) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing of the Statement on Schedule 13D to which this Joint Filing Agreement is attached, and any amendments thereto may be filed without the necessity of filing additional joint filing agreements.
perturbation.changed_text: Pursuant and subject to Rule 13d-1(k)(1) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing of the Statement on Schedule 13D to which this Joint Filing Agreement is attached. Any amendments thereto must be filed with additional joint filing agreements signed by all parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that amendments may be filed without additional joint filing agreements, and the modified text explicitly requires additional joint filing agreements for each amendment. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the procedure for filing amendments and whether additional agreements are required, introducing uncertainty about the amendment process.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Pursuant and subject to Rule 13d-1(k)(1) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing of the Statement on Schedule 13D to which this Joint Filing Agreement is attached. Any amendments thereto must be filed with additional joint filing agreements signed by all parties. This Joint Filing Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, but all of which shall constitute one and the same instrument. The execution and filing of this agreement shall not be construed as an admission that the below-named parties are a group or have acted as a group.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly requires additional joint filing agreements for each amendment to Schedule 13D. This directly contradicts both the original text and the contradicted text, which, in their original forms, either permitted filing amendments without additional agreements or were ambiguous about whether they were needed. The changed text introduces a structural flaw (in-text contradiction) by creating directly conflicting obligations regarding the amendment process, creating an inconsistency as per the prompt's definitions.

perturbation.original_text: The execution and filing of this agreement shall not be construed as an admission that the below-named parties are a group or have acted as a group.
perturbation.changed_text: The execution and filing of this agreement shall be construed as an admission that the below-named parties are a group and have acted as a group for the purpose of this filing.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that the agreement should not be construed as an admission of group activity, whereas the modified text states the opposite – that it *is* an admission of group activity. This creates a fundamental conflict in understanding the purpose and implications of signing the agreement. It introduces uncertainty about the legal standing of all parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Pursuant and subject to Rule 13d-1(k)(1) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing of the Statement on Schedule 13D to which this Joint Filing Agreement is attached. Any amendments thereto must be filed with additional joint filing agreements signed by all parties. This Joint Filing Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, but all of which shall constitute one and the same instrument. The execution and filing of this agreement shall be construed as an admission that the below-named parties are a group and have acted as a group for the purpose of this filing.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly denies that the agreement is an admission of group activity, while the changed text states the opposite: that it *is* an admission of group activity for the purpose of filing. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text. Therefore, since the original text and the changed text are direct opposites, the change causes a strong contradiction in the document. This falls under Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Joint Filing Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, but all of which shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.changed_text: This Joint Filing Agreement must be executed as a single original document, and counterparts are not permitted.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows for the agreement to be executed in any number of counterparts, each considered an original. The modified text directly contradicts this by stating that the agreement *must* be a single original, explicitly forbidding counterparts. This creates uncertainty about the proper execution method of the agreement, thus creating in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Pursuant and subject to Rule 13d-1(k)(1) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing of the Statement on Schedule 13D to which this Joint Filing Agreement is attached. Any amendments thereto must be filed with additional joint filing agreements signed by all parties. This Joint Filing Agreement must be executed as a single original document, and counterparts are not permitted. The execution and filing of this agreement shall be construed as an admission that the below-named parties are a group and have acted as a group for the purpose of this filing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states "This Joint Filing Agreement must be executed as a single original document, and counterparts are not permitted." This directly contradicts the original text which allowed for multiple counterparts, each considered an original. Furthermore, this changed text is also present in the contradicted text, creating a direct, in-text contradiction. The added restriction on single original documents directly contradicts the previous allowance of counterparts. This creates uncertainty about the proper execution method of the agreement, thus creating in-text contradiction.
